name: Chapchae
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the Chinese black mushrooms in 1 cup hot water for 20 minutes.
- When they have softened, cut off the hard stems and slice the caps fine.
2. Drop the spinach into 10 cups of boiling water.
- Boil rapidly for 2 minutes.
- Drain and run under cold water.
- Squeeze out as much moisture as possible.
3. Peel the carrot and cut into 3 sections, then into fine julienne strips.
4. Wipe off the fresh mushrooms and break off their stems.
- Cut the caps into very fine slices.
- Cut the nonwoody part of the stems into matchstick pieces.
5. Cut away and discard the curly, tender part of the cabbage leaves.
- Save only the V-shaped core of the leaves and cut it into julienne strips.
6. Cut the scallions into 2-1/2-inch sections and quarter the section with the bulb lengthwise.
7. Combine all vegetables in a bowl, separating the spinach leaves.
8. Heat the vegetable oil and sesame oil in a wok or saute pan over medium-high heat.
- When hot, add the garlic and stir for 10 seconds.
- Add the vegetables from the bowl and stir fry for 3 to 4 minutes until tender-crisp.
- Turn off the heat.
9. Add the drained noodles, soy sauce, and salt. Stir well, distributing the noodles evenly, and cook for 2 to 3 minutes.
- Taste for seasonings.
